plan_title: High-Qualified Leads Filter + Dual Email Notification
plan_description: Filters a Google Sheet of leads to those marked as high qualified (Stage = 4), builds an HTML summary table, and sends two separate email notifications to end users. If there are no matching leads, it emails the group manager.

data: - Read lead rows from the Google Sheet with ID "1pM8WbXtPgaYqokHn_spgQAfR7SBuql3JUtE1ugDtOpc".
- Use the lead status field described as "column stage = 4" to determine whether a lead is high qualified.
- Treat the status match rule as: the status field contains the value "4" (because you selected contains match).
actions: - Filter the Sheet rows to only those where the Stage/status field contains "4".
- Build a summary table from the filtered rows.
- Keep the summary table columns in this exact order: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person, Source.
- If there are zero matching rows after filtering, do not send the normal end-user emails; instead, send a no high qualified leads found email to the group manager.
output: - Format the summary as an HTML table embedded directly in the email body (not as an attachment).
- Include one row per matching lead.
- Include a short header line above the table stating that these are the leads with Stage = 4 (high qualified).
delivery: - Send two separate emails (one per recipient) with the same subject and HTML body.
- Send the end-user emails to: meiribarak@gmail.com and eomer3@gmail.com.
- If there are zero matching leads, send the no results email to avital.livovsky@gmail.com.
processing_steps: - Load all rows from the specified Sheet tab.
- Identify the column that represents Stage/status.
- Apply the contains 4 filter to that column.
- Select and order the requested output columns.
- Render the selected rows as an HTML table.
- Send emails according to the delivery rules (two separate emails, or manager-only email when there are no matches).
